items.title: Michael becomes the highest-grossing biopic of all time — despite criticisms against limited scope
items.summary_auto: Despite middling reviews, strong competition and even late rewrites and legal hurdles, the Jackson family film Michael has become the highest-grossing biopic of all time.

items.title: Is Canada's 'technical recession' already over?
items.summary_auto: Is Canada's so-called "technical recession" already over? Canada's economy likely grew by 0.4 per cent in April.

items.title: Has Canada's GDP bounced back?
items.summary_auto: Is Canada's so-called "technical recession" already over? Canada's economy likely grew by 0.4 per cent in April.

items.title: Akwesasne man pleads guilty in smuggling case that left family with 2 toddlers dead
items.summary_auto: A 31-year-old man pleaded guilty last week for his role in a human smuggling conspiracy that led to the 2023 drowning deaths of a family, including two children under the age of three, in the St. Lawrence River.

items.title: Crews have 'exhausted all possibilities' of finding survivors in search for 6 missing off B.C. coast
Murder-suicide leaves 2 boys and their father dead, Ottawa police say
items.summary_auto: Ottawa and provincial police are investigating a murder-suicide that has left two children and their father dead.

items.title: U.S. Supreme Court rejects Trump-led challenge against mail-in ballots
items.summary_auto: The U.S. Supreme Court on Monday ruled that states can count ballots that arrive after election day, a persistent target of President Donald Trump.

items.title: Petition signature counting resumes as Alberta separatist group gets partial win at court of appeal
items.summary_auto: An Alberta separatist group has secured a partial win at the province’s top court after a lower court judge quashed its referendum petition last month.

items.title: Italy, Balkans latest in Europe to feel brunt of record-breaking heat wave
items.summary_auto: Italy and the Balkans felt the impact on Monday of a record-breaking heat wave that has caused hundreds of excess deaths and disrupted daily life across the continent for more than a week, with growing concerns over the ‌spread of wildfires.

items.title: 6 killed in shooting at shelter for mothers, children in Germany
items.summary_auto: Police said three people were detained, including the suspected ​shooter, and that all the fatalities were adults.

items.title: These planets are 1,000 times bigger than Earth, but lighter than cotton candy: study
items.summary_auto: About 1,113 light years away from Earth, locked in orbit around a single star, there are two planets that appear to be the size of Jupiter, but have the density of "a nice blob of shaving foam, fresh from the can."

items.title: Ever heard Bohemian Rhapsody sung in ... Minionese? Viral clip has fans going bananas
items.summary_auto: People are going, shall we say, bananas on social media over TikTok creator Ella Mott's rendition of Bohemian Rhapsody at Hollywood's Dolby Theatre on Sunday night during the premiere of Minions & Monsters, the latest installment in Illumination's Despicable Me franchise.

items.title: Montreal Canadiens trade Brendan Gallagher to Canucks
items.summary_auto: The Vancouver Canucks have acquired forward Brendan Gallagher from the Montreal Canadiens for future considerations.

items.title: Unfortunate typo prompts removal of French-language posters from downtown Ottawa
items.summary_auto: An unfortunate typo on French-language posters promoting a public space improvement program has prompted their swift removal from Ottawa's downtown.

items.title: Tom Siddon, cabinet minister during Oka Crisis, dies at 84
items.summary_auto: Tom Siddon, who served as a cabinet minister under Prime Minister Brian Mulroney during the Oka crisis and later helped to establish Nunavut, has died at 84.

items.title: 'Monster' tornado destroys century-old brick farmhouse in western Manitoba
items.summary_auto: Severe thunderstorms on Sunday swept through beleaguered western Manitoba — still recovering from storms earlier this month — bringing at least one tornado that a storm chaser called a "monster."

items.title: High heat and humidity forecast for southern Quebec and Ontario
items.summary_auto: Environment Canada is warning about an extended period of hot and humid weather forecast for southern Quebec and Ontario this week.

items.title: U.S. Supreme Court rules against Trump bid to fire Lisa Cook, Federal Reserve governor
items.summary_auto: The U.S. Supreme Court refused on Monday ​to let Donald Trump fire Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook as it stood firm to preserve the central bank's cherished independence against an unprecedented challenge by the Republican president.

items.title: Former NBA players Malik Beasley, Ed Davis latest to be charged in gambling scandal
items.summary_auto: Ex-NBA players Malik Beasley and Ed Davis have been indicted in the latest round of charges in the government's gambling investigation, authorities said Monday.

items.title: IN PHOTOS | Pride parades here and abroad
items.summary_auto: Pride Month celebrations peaked Sunday with big parades in Toronto, San Francisco and other major cities on the anniversary of the 1969 Stonewall uprising, which accelerated and transformed the modern LGBTQ+ rights movement.

items.title: U.S. Supreme Court declines Trump's appeal in E. Jean Carroll case
items.summary_auto: The U.S. Supreme Court declined on Monday to hear President Donald Trump's bid to overturn a $5-million US verdict in favor of E. Jean Carroll.

items.title: Wrigley, N.W.T., residents ordered to evacuate due to nearby wildfire
items.summary_auto: A wildfire near the small community of Wrigley, N.W.T., grew substantially over the weekend. Pedzéh Kı̨ First Nation Chief Jamie Moses told CBC that residents will evacuate by plane to Fort Smith Monday afternoon.

items.title: NBA star Gilgeous-Alexander headlines Canada roster ahead of FIBA Americas qualifiers
items.summary_auto: Two-time NBA MVP ShaiGilgeous-Alexander was the biggest name added to Canada's roster for the upcoming FIBA Men's Basketball World Cup 2027 Americas Qualifiers.

items.title: First wildfire evacuees from Fort Simpson, N.W.T., arrive in Yellowknife
items.summary_auto: Firefighters continue to tackle an out-of-control blaze on the outskirts of the Dehcho community, as many of the community’s 1,300 residents are evacuating to Yellowknife.

items.title: 2 years ago a boy died of rabies from a bat. Doctors are reminding the public to be aware
items.summary_auto: In the latest issue of the Canadian Medical Association Journal, infectious disease physicians described the case of an 11-year-old child who died of rabies almost two years ago, and urged people to seek medical attention if they have any contact with bats to prevent potential rabies infection.

items.title: Russian strikes across Ukraine kill at least 9, injure dozens
items.summary_auto: Russian attacks across Ukraine killed nine people and wounded dozens on Monday, authorities said, with strikes continuing into the afternoon as the death toll climbed.

items.title: Australia proposes increasing fines for social media companies in under-16 ban
items.summary_auto: Australia introduced new legislation in Parliament on Monday to strengthen enforcement of its under-16 ‌social media ban and give its internet regulator more power to pursue tech giants in court for non-compliance.

items.title: Canadian tries to set world record for most trees planted in 24 hours — again
items.summary_auto: The last time Kenny Chaplin broke the Guinness World Record for most trees planted in 24 hours, the year was 2001 and he was a much younger man. He's doing it all again in 2026 at the village of Buena Vista, Sask.

items.title: Top physicians warned Winnipeg hospitals nearing 'disintegration' in March letter
items.summary_auto: Top Winnipeg doctors warned in a confidential letter in March that urgent-care centres might have to close and another patient death was inevitable unless immediate changes were made.

items.title: New research suggests invasive spotted lanternfly may be able to survive Canadian winters
items.summary_auto: Research out of the Great Lakes Forestry Centre in Sault Ste. Marie, Ont., is raising new concerns about an invasive insect that has been steadily moving closer to the Canadian border.

items.title: Family of teen fatally injured on Quebec ski lift grieves a life that 'shouldn't be over'
Ottawa relaunches federal green home retrofit program in 4 provinces
items.summary_auto: After a hiatus, the popular greener homes  program is returning to Nova Scotia, Prince Edward Island, Quebec and British Columbia.

items.title: 'Feels like checkmate': Cubans chart risky path forward under U.S. pressure
items.summary_auto: Six months into a tumultuous year for Cuba, the winds of change are aggressively blowing across the Straits of Florida, toward the island nation.

items.title: Apartments can become sweltering in summer. Why heat bylaws could be coming to a city near you
items.summary_auto: As temperatures and deaths connected to heat keep climbing, more tenants, politicians and climate advocates are calling for cooling rules. During municipal debates — from councils in British Columbia to Newfoundland — the same question keeps coming up: Who should pay for the upgrades?

items.title: Some Venezuelans fear a dark and dangerous future in aftermath of twin earthquakes
items.summary_auto: The future seems dark and dangerous for some Venezuelans amid the dust, debris and death shrouding parts of the country that suffered the brunt of twin earthquakes, creating one of the worst natural disasters in its history.

items.title: As winds of change blow toward Cuba, how will Havana chart a path forward?
items.summary_auto: Six months into a tumultuous year for Cuba, the winds of change are aggressively blowing across the Straits of Florida, toward the island nation.

items.title: Venezuelans angry over lack of earthquake preparedness
items.summary_auto: As crews continue to search through the rubble for potential survivors, many Venezuelans expressed anger at their government for its lack of disaster preparedness after years of political corruption.

items.title: Search underway for convicted killer believed to have escaped from Vancouver Island prison
items.summary_auto: A search is underway for a convicted killer police describe as "dangerous," who is believed to have escaped from a federal prison on Vancouver Island.
